description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, featuring two prominent, slightly weathered white metal hoops encircling its top and bottom. The wood staves are vertically aligned and display a natural, dark brown grain with visible signs of wear and aging, including some scuffs and faded patches. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top appearing to be securely closed. The metal hoops show some rust and chipping, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the barrel appears to be solidly constructed for storage or transport. Its rustic appearance suggests it could be used for decorative or practical purposes in urban outdoor settings such as parks, alleys, or courtyards.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with white metal hoops and a closed top, showing signs of age and outdoor use. The surface is rustic with visible wood grain and some scuff marks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel's closed wooden top, white metal hoops, and a patch of scuffed wood on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the even spacing of the metal hoops and a relatively clean wood surface with minor wear.
description_view_2: Rear view with a large faded patch on the wood and clear visibility of the top and bottom hoops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the most pronounced weathering on the wood and the full circular outline of the top.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
